question_text: For 𝑌∈ℝ𝑛, 𝑋∈ℝ𝑛×𝑝, and 𝛽∈ℝ𝑝, consider a regression model 𝑌= 𝑋 𝛽+ 𝜖, where 𝜖 has an 𝑛-dimensional multivariate normal distribution with zero mean vector and identity covariance matrix. Let 𝐼𝑝 denote the identity matrix of order 𝑝. For 𝜆> 0, let 𝛽̂𝑛= (𝑋𝑇𝑋+ 𝜆𝐼𝑝) −1𝑋𝑇𝑌,

options.B: e an estimator of 𝛽. Then which of the following options is/are correct?
options.A: 𝛽̂𝑛 is an unbiased estimator of 𝛽
options.C: 𝛽̂𝑛 has a multivariate normal distribution
